title: Vice
logline: The rise of Dick Cheney from a drunken Wyoming lineman to the most powerful Vice President in American history, chronicling his transformation and the far-reaching consequences of his political machinations.
antagonistOrConflict: The corrupting nature of unchecked political power and its impact on American democracy
tone: Satirical, cynical, and darkly comedic with moments of genuine drama
themes: The cost of ambition on family and democracy

actBreaks.description: Cheney enters the Oval Office under Ford, marking his first real taste of executive power and setting up his lifelong pursuit of it.

validation.ruling: The absurdist Oval Office scene with Ford, Kissinger, Rumsfeld and Cheney represents a major tonal and narrative shift that fundamentally changes the story's direction, marking Cheney's entry into the highest levels of power and establishing the irreversible trajectory toward his political ascension.

actBreaks.description: 9/11 occurs, providing Cheney with the crisis he needs to implement his vision of expanded executive power and launch the War on Terror.

validation.ruling: The 9/11 scene where Cheney watches the second plane hit the towers and is rushed to the bunker represents the ultimate catalyst that transforms him from a behind-the-scenes operator into the most powerful Vice President in history, fundamentally changing the story's stakes and direction.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: McKay employs a deliberately fragmented, satirical approach that mirrors the secretive, compartmentalized nature of Cheney's career. The script jumps between time periods with theatrical flourishes (like the fake ending at scene 72) and meta-commentary from an unreliable narrator. This non-linear structure serves the film's thesis that Cheney's influence was often hidden and indirect, requiring a kaleidoscopic view to understand his true impact. The pacing accelerates dramatically after 9/11, reflecting how that crisis became the catalyst for Cheney's most consequential actions.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The first act moves leisurely through Cheney's early years and political apprenticeship, with longer scenes establishing character. Post-9/11 (scenes 100-175), the pacing becomes frenetic with rapid-fire montages and shorter scenes, mirroring the urgency of the War on Terror era. The fake ending at scene 72 serves as a structural palate cleanser before the VP selection process accelerates the story toward its climax.

incitingIncident.description: Lynne furiously confronts Dick about his second arrest, threatening to leave him if he doesn't change his ways from drinking and fighting.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. LYNNE’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - SAME TIME

validation.ruling: Lynne's ultimatum to Dick in her living room shatters his status quo and forces him to choose between transformation or losing her, establishing the main conflict of his character arc and creating irreversible stakes that drive the entire narrative forward.

midpoint.description: At Crawford Ranch, Bush directly asks Cheney to be his VP, and Cheney responds that while the Vice Presidency is mostly symbolic, it's also defined by the President and could be expanded.

validation.ruling: Position sanity: original at 64.2%. Corrected to nearest turning-point in 35-72% window.

climax.description: Cheney dismisses public opinion about the Iraq War and addresses the camera directly, defending his actions and refusing to apologize for his decisions.

validation.ruling: The stock footage of Baghdad bombing lacks dramatic confrontation and character resolution. Scene #174 in the interview room represents the ultimate confrontation where Cheney directly faces questions about his actions and demonstrates his unrepentant stance, resolving the main story question about his character and applying lessons from his arc.

scenes.heading: INT. ROAD SIDE BAR WYOMING - 1963 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Young Dick Cheney, drunk and celebrating, plays dice in a Wyoming roadside bar filled with rough locals.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESOLATE ROAD - NIGHT - LATER
scenes.summary: Dick drives drunk and erratically before being pulled over by police, stumbling to the ground when asked to exit his car.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE OFFICE OF VICE PRESIDENT - 9/11 2001 - DAY
scenes.summary: Vice President Cheney watches the second plane hit the World Trade Center on 9/11 before Secret Service rushes him to the underground bunker.

scenes.heading: INT. HAIR SALON - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Lynne Cheney watches 9/11 coverage on television in a hair salon, staring in shock at the twin towers.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDERGROUND WHITE HOUSE BUNKER - 9/11 9:38AM
scenes.summary: Cheney takes command in the White House bunker during 9/11, coordinating with staff and advising the President to stay airborne.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVER - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Cheney fly fishes alone in a river, working his rod and line repeatedly.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH SCHOOL FOOTBALL FIELD - HOMECOMING
scenes.summary: Young Lynne Cheney waves from a car as homecoming queen in a sparkled cowboy outfit.

scenes.heading: INT. YALE DORM ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Cheney lies passed out in his Yale dorm room, surrounded by vomit on his sheets and floor.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAST WYOMING FIELD - DAY - 1963
scenes.summary: Dick works as a lineman on power lines when he hears a man scream and looks down to see an accident below.

scenes.heading: EXT. POWER LINE BASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Linemen gather around a fallen worker with a broken leg bone protruding from his pants while the boss orders everyone back to work.

scenes.heading: INT. ROAD SIDE BAR WYOMING 1963 - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dick drinks and dances at the bar when a shifty crew member provokes him, leading Dick to shove and punch the man.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESOLATE ROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dick stumbles out of his car with police assistance after being pulled over for drunk driving.

scenes.heading: EXT. LYNNE’S HOUSE - THE NEXT MORNING
scenes.summary: A dog barks in the distance outside Lynne's lower middle class home the morning after Dick's arrest.

scenes.heading: INT. LYNNE’S HOUSE - LIVING ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Lynne furiously confronts Dick about his second arrest, threatening to leave him if he doesn't change his ways from drinking and fighting.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lynne's ultimatum forces Dick to confront his self-destructive behavior and choose between his current path and his future with her

scenes.heading: INT. RAYBURN HOUSE OFFICE BUILDING - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney sits bored among congressional fellows listening to a monotone congressman before Donald Rumsfeld takes the stage.

scenes.heading: INT. PRINCETON WRESTLING GYM - DAY - 1952
scenes.summary: Super 8 footage shows young Donald Rumsfeld posing in wrestling gear, ready for a takedown.

scenes.heading: EXT. NAVY AIR FIELD - DAY - 1956
scenes.summary: Rumsfeld gives an energetic speech to congressional fellows about the opportunity to work in the halls of power, warning them not to work for boring colleagues.

scenes.heading: INT. RUMSFELD’S OFFICE - MONTHS LATER
scenes.summary: Cheney reports to Rumsfeld's office for his first day, receiving brusque instructions about his duties before Rumsfeld dismisses him.

scenes.heading: INT. CONGRESSIONAL FLOOR
scenes.summary: Cheney works various tasks for Rumsfeld, including receiving a memo from Roger Ailes about starting a Republican TV news network.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Rumsfeld walks down a White House hallway explaining to Cheney the importance of loyalty, keeping quiet, and following orders.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE - CHENEY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney enters his tiny windowless office and picks up his phone as the narrator begins to introduce himself.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDDLE AMERICA HOME - 2011 - DAY
scenes.summary: The narrator Kurt introduces himself as a father who loves football and SpongeBob, hinting at his connection to Dick Cheney.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE CHENEY’S OFFICE
scenes.summary: Dick calls Lynne from his White House office, beaming with pride about his new position.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL APARTMENT IN D.C. - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Lynne talks to Dick on the phone while holding baby Mary, expressing pride in his achievement and sharing his excitement about meeting Nixon.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Rumsfeld stops Cheney in a White House hallway to explain that Nixon is secretly meeting with Kissinger about bombing Cambodia.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAMBODIAN VILLAGE - DAY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: A peaceful Cambodian village goes about daily life as bombs whistle overhead, followed by massive explosions.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY - RUMSFELD’S OFFICE - LATER
scenes.summary: Cheney asks Rumsfeld what they believe politically, causing Rumsfeld to laugh hysterically before slamming his door and leaving Cheney alone.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK YARD - BETHESDA, MARYLAND - 1973
scenes.summary: Dick teaches his daughters to fish with worms, explaining that it's neither good nor bad, just fishing.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY’S BEDROOM - NIGHT - 1973
scenes.summary: Rumsfeld calls Dick and Lynne in the middle of the night to tell them he's being sent to Brussels as NATO ambassador.

scenes.heading: INT. RUMSFELD’S OFFICE - SAME TIME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rumsfeld tears up papers in his office while asking Dick to come with him to Brussels, but Dick declines due to his family.

scenes.heading: EXT. BACK YARD - BETHESDA, MARYLAND - 1973
scenes.summary: Lynne walks across the backyard looking ashen and tells Dick that something terrible has happened back home in Casper.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE IN CASPER, WYOMING - DAY
scenes.summary: Police cars arrive at a lake where Lynne's mother Edna was found drowned, with her car doors open and belongings scattered.

scenes.heading: EXT. CHENEY HOME IN MARYLAND 1973
scenes.summary: Dick holds Lynne in his arms as she expresses shock that her mother, who never swam, was found drowned.

scenes.heading: EXT. GRAVE YARD - FUNERAL - DAY
scenes.summary: At Edna's funeral, mourners listen to a priest as the coffin is lowered, with Dick later confronting Wayne about the circumstances.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE OFFICE - 2003 - MORNING
scenes.summary: Vice President Cheney takes the best pastry from a breakfast spread and asks CIA agents about their classified operations.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET IN MILAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Three men in black kidnap a Muslim cleric from a Milan street, beating him and putting a hood over his bloodied face.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - 2003 - MORNING
scenes.summary: Cheney asks for the next name on the CIA's list as they discuss targeting an engineering student in Berlin.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY’S LIVING ROOM - 1974 - DAY
scenes.summary: The Cheney family watches Nixon resign on television while Lynne warns Liz that powerful people always have enemies trying to take their power.

scenes.heading: EXT. DULLES PARKING LOT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Rumsfeld gets into Cheney's car at the airport parking lot as Nixon's resignation plays on television.

scenes.heading: INT. VW BUG - PENNSYLVANIA AVE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Cheney and Rumsfeld smoke in the car while driving to the White House, with Dick declaring their plan is to take over the place.

scenes.heading: INT. RUMSFELD’S NEW OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Rumsfeld celebrates becoming Chief of Staff by pouring drinks in his new office while discussing plans to counter Kissinger's Soviet diplomacy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rumsfeld's appointment as Chief of Staff marks the beginning of his and Cheney's rise to real power in the Ford administration

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - WHITE HOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: In the Oval Office, Cheney makes an absurd sexual suggestion about dealing with Russia that Ford and others treat as normal policy discussion.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY’S NEW OFFICE - DAY - NOVEMBER, 1975
scenes.summary: Lynne celebrates Dick becoming the youngest Chief of Staff in history in his new White House office before their daughters are brought in by Secret Service.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cheney's promotion to Chief of Staff represents his ascension to the highest levels of executive power

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE OF LEGAL COUNSEL - DOJ - THE NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney meets with a young Justice Department lawyer who introduces him to the theory of the unitary executive.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cheney's introduction to unitary executive theory provides the constitutional framework that will guide his later expansion of executive power

scenes.heading: INT. MIDDLE AMERICA HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: The narrator explains the unitary executive theory to his family, describing how it gives presidents the power of kings and dictators.

scenes.heading: INT. RESIDENTIAL QUARTERS - WHITE HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ford, Cheney, Rumsfeld and their wives watch election returns showing Carter's victory, ending their time in power.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Republican loss forces Cheney out of the White House and into his next phase seeking elected office

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE ROOF - 1976
scenes.summary: Workers install solar panels on the White House roof as President Carter announces his solar energy initiative.

scenes.heading: EXT. WYOMING RIVER - 2012 - DAY
scenes.summary: An older, unsteady Cheney trudges through a Wyoming river with fishing gear in slow motion.

scenes.heading: EXT. SMALL TOWN IN WYOMING - DAY - SUMMER - 1978
scenes.summary: A beat-up Winnebago with a 'Cheney for Congress' sign sits parked outside a VFW in a small Wyoming town.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL TOWN VFW - DAY - SUMMER - 1978
scenes.summary: Cheney gives an awkward speech about taxes to ranchers at a VFW while Lynne coaches from the back and his campaign manager notes his poor performance.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney lies in a hospital bed with heart monitors after suffering an inferior wall infarct, while a doctor tells him and Lynne he needs two weeks of bed rest and should drop out of the election.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANCH - DAY
scenes.summary: Lynne fills in for the hospitalized Dick at a ranch campaign event, taking the microphone off its stand when she's too short to reach it and giving a speech to hard-faced farmers and ranchers.

scenes.heading: EXT. WYOMING RURAL AREA
scenes.summary: A van weaves through a windy dirt road in rural Wyoming while Lynne's voiceover speaks about believing in right and wrong.

scenes.heading: INT. CHURCH BASEMENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Lynne gives a speech to a Ladies Auxiliary Meeting in a church basement, speaking as a mom and wife from Wyoming while the narrator describes conservative resistance to social changes.

scenes.heading: INT. DC RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dick and Lynne toast with congressmen and their wives at a DC restaurant, celebrating Dick's election as Wyoming's sole Congressional Representative.

scenes.heading: EXT. DC STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: Old money patriarchs get out of limos in slow motion as the narrator explains how wealthy families funded right-wing think tanks to change American perspectives.

scenes.heading: INT. THINK TANK OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: The camera reveals a giant office at the American Enterprise Institute with dozens of people working at desks, with floating text showing their conservative research topics.

scenes.heading: INT. RNC CONVENTION - DETROIT - 1980
scenes.summary: Stock footage shows Ronald Reagan giving his actual nomination speech at the 1980 RNC Convention in Detroit, promising to make America great again.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reagan's rise marks the beginning of the conservative revolution that will define Cheney's political ascent

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE ROOF - 1980 - DAY
scenes.summary: Workers unscrew solar panels from the White House roof and discard them into a pile.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - CAPITOL BUILDING - DAY - 1980’S
scenes.summary: Cheney walks confidently through Capitol Building hallways talking to congressmen and aides as the narrator declares it's the 1980s and a hell of a time to be Dick Cheney.

scenes.heading: INT. FLOOR OF CONGRESS - DAY
scenes.summary: Quick cuts show Cheney inserting his House voting card and voting 'nay' against various acts including gun control, environmental protection, and the Martin Luther King Jr. federal holiday.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - CAPITOL BUILDING - DAY - 1984
scenes.summary: Dick collapses in a Capitol Building hallway, going to his knees, and tells a panicked aide he's having a heart attack.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE - EAST WING - NIGHT - 1986
scenes.summary: Dick and Lynne enter a White House Christmas party like royalty, with on-screen text showing their new prestigious positions as House Minority Whip and Chair of the National Endowment for the Humanities.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWSROOM - MODERN DAY
scenes.summary: A blonde news anchor explains how the conservative movement eventually led to Roger Ailes' dream of Fox News, which would dominate news and swing America further right.

scenes.heading: INT. CHRISTMAS PARTY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The party unfreezes as a lanky drunk man crashes into champagne glasses, prompting George Bush Sr. to usher him out while Lynne identifies him as Bush's black sheep son George W.

scenes.heading: EXT. VIRGINIA HIGH SCHOOL - DAY
scenes.summary: 17-year-old Mary Cheney leaves school sobbing, ignores a teacher's calls to return, jumps in her car and speeds through a stop sign before being hit by another car.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - AN HOUR LATER
scenes.summary: In the hospital, Mary tells her parents she's gay after explaining that her girlfriend Susan broke up with her, and Dick moves to hug her saying they will always love her no matter what.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mary's coming out creates a personal conflict with the Cheneys' conservative political positions that will influence major future decisions

scenes.heading: INT. SHIPPING WAREHOUSE - 2011 - DAY
scenes.summary: Kurt the narrator drives a pallet cart in a shipping warehouse while explaining that Cheney became Secretary of Defense under Bush Sr. and later considered running for President.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY KITCHEN - DAY - 1992
scenes.summary: Cheney holds early poll results showing him dead last behind 11 other Republicans and tells Lynne 'that's it,' while she suggests they can move the numbers by attacking welfare and regulations.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIDDLE EASTERN DESERT - DAY - 1993
scenes.summary: Cheney walks across a Middle Eastern desert with the family of Saud as on-screen text reveals he became CEO of Halliburton.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cheney's transition to private sector CEO of Halliburton marks his temporary exit from politics

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE - HOUSTON SUBURB - 1993
scenes.summary: The extended Cheney family fishes and relaxes by a lake in Houston, with on-screen text explaining Dick chose his daughter over the Presidency and that the Cheneys would never again enter politics.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY HOME OFFICE - 1999- DAY
scenes.summary: Dick answers a Sunday morning phone call at home and tells Lynne that someone from George W. Bush's campaign wants to talk to him about being his running mate.
scenes.turningPointNote: The call from Bush's campaign pulls Cheney back into politics despite his apparent retirement

scenes.heading: EXT. AUSTIN, TEXAS - CAPITOL BUILDING - DAY - 1999
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of the Austin, Texas Capitol Building.

scenes.heading: INT. GOVERNOR’S OFFICE - LOBBY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Cheney sits in the Governor's office lobby reading a Time Magazine with Bush on the cover before a receptionist tells him the Governor will see him now.

scenes.heading: INT. GOVERNOR’S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: George W. Bush and Cheney meet in the Governor's office, discussing foreign policy sessions and agreeing that Bush Sr. would have been re-elected if he had taken out Saddam Hussein.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY’S BATHROOM - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dick and Lynne prepare for bed in their bathroom, with Dick telling Lynne that Bush is 'green' and that he told him he'd help with the VP search, while the narrator questions what Cheney is really thinking.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY BEDROOM
scenes.summary: Dick and Lynne get into bed and speak in Shakespearean verse about power, ambition, and their shared bond, with Lynne encouraging Dick's political aspirations.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY KITCHEN - THE NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: The Cheney family and friend David Gribbin work on the VP questionnaire in the kitchen, with Dick insisting they look under every stone and David explaining the comprehensive nature of the vetting process.

scenes.heading: INT. LAW OFFICE - VIRGINIA - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: David Addington explains to Dick over the phone that the Vice Presidency exists in both executive and legislative branches, making it part of neither, which could provide unique constitutional powers.

scenes.heading: EXT. CRAWFORD RANCH - DAY
scenes.summary: At Crawford Ranch, Bush directly asks Cheney to be his VP, and Cheney responds that while the Vice Presidency is mostly symbolic, it's also defined by the President and could be expanded.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cheney accepts the VP role with the understanding that he can redefine and expand its powers

scenes.heading: INT. FRANK ERWIN CENTER - AUSTIN - 2000 - DAY
scenes.summary: Dick and Lynne wait backstage before being introduced as Bush's running mate and his wife at a campaign event, with the narrator noting Dick never filled out his own questionnaire.

scenes.heading: INT. FOUR SEASONS - AUSTIN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: On election night, Scooter Libby tells Cheney that Gore has rescinded his concession and Florida will have a recount, prompting Cheney to decide they should act like they've already won.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY KITCHEN - A WEEK LATER - DAY
scenes.summary: The Cheney team works from the kitchen during the recount, with Dick deciding on cabinet appointments including Rumsfeld as Secretary of Defense and Wolfowitz for State or Pentagon.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPREME COURT - DAY
scenes.summary: The Supreme Court convenes as the narrator explains that Justice Scalia and the Court stopped Florida's recount, making Bush and Cheney the winners.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Supreme Court decision definitively puts Bush and Cheney in the White House

scenes.heading: EXT. WASHINGTON DC. 2001 - DAY
scenes.summary: An overhead shot shows a procession of five black limousines driving into the heart of Washington DC.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cheney walks alone through White House halls and stops at the closed Oval Office door, looking inside while flashing back to his first day in the Nixon White House calling Lynne.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE OF THE VICE PRESIDENT - A FEW DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Cheney's team including Scooter, Addington, Matalin, Wolfowitz, and Rumsfeld gather in the Vice President's office as Scooter begins explaining their organizational structure.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - WHITE HOUSE
scenes.summary: Using game pieces, Scooter explains how Cheney's people will surround Bush by having dual roles, with Bush's advisors being swept off the board and replaced by Cheney allies.
scenes.turningPointNote: The visual representation shows Cheney's systematic plan to control the Bush administration

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAYS - LATER
scenes.summary: Dick and Rumsfeld walk through White House hallways as Dick advises a softer touch, but reveals he has an office at the House of Representatives, not just the Senate.

scenes.heading: EXT. WASHINGTON D.C. - DAY
scenes.summary: Quick flashes show a car racing around Capitol Hill as the narrator explains Cheney got an office at the House through Dennis Hastert to be near the money faucet.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAYS OF THE CAPITOL - DAY
scenes.summary: Fast tracking shots through Capitol halls show Dennis Hastert and Trent Lott giving Cheney offices in both the House and Senate, plus one at the Pentagon.

scenes.heading: INT. BIG CONFERENCE ROOM WITH PODIUM AT THE FRONT - DAY
scenes.summary: Dick and Lynne enter Grover Norquist's Wednesday meeting where hundreds of GOP congressmen, lobbyists and right-wing media stand for them as Norquist discusses eliminating the estate tax.

scenes.heading: INT. FOCUS GROUP ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Frank Luntz conducts focus groups showing how rebranding 'estate tax' as 'death tax' and 'global warming' as 'climate change' dramatically shifts public opinion.

scenes.heading: EXT. LINE OF CARS STOPPED AT A RED LIGHT - DAY
scenes.summary: Cars stopped at a red light play various radio shows including right-wing talk about the 'death tax' while web pages show coordinated conservative messaging.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDDLE AMERICA HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Kurt the narrator plays with his son at home while death tax propaganda plays on TV, as the narrator explains Cheney's success in cutting taxes and gutting regulations.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWSROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A news anchor introduces Cheney's National Energy Policy Development Group as his first major test to expand executive power.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE OF THE VICE PRESIDENT - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney, Libby and Addington discuss the Federal Advisory Committees Act that requires government officials present when CEOs meet with elected officials, with Addington finding a loophole.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE MEETING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. ENVIRONMENTAL GROUP’S HEADQUARTERS - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE OFFICE OF VICE PRESIDENT - 9/11 2001 - DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. HAIR SALON - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Lynne Cheney argues with Secret Service agents at a hair salon, demanding they take her to the White House despite their safety concerns as news reports show the Pentagon has been hit.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDERGROUND WHITE HOUSE BUNKER - 9/11 9:38AM
scenes.summary: In the White House bunker during 9/11, Cheney authorizes shooting down aircraft deemed threats and gives Rumsfeld orders marked 'UNODIR' (unless otherwise directed), causing concern from Condi Rice about proper rules of engagement.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cheney assumes unprecedented executive authority during the crisis, setting the stage for his expanded power grab

scenes.heading: INT. UNDERGROUND BUNKER - WHITE HOUSE - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: In the bunker that night, intelligence officials brief the leadership that Al Qaeda operatives are celebrating the attack, while Rumsfeld pushes to target Iraq despite Richard Clarke insisting Iraq has nothing to do with it.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE ROOF - HOURS LATER
scenes.summary: Secret Service agents watch as Cheney's helicopter takes off from the White House roof, with one agent noting that normally only the President can use the south lawn.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDERGROUND TUNNEL - UNDISCLOSED LOCATION - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dick and Lynne Cheney travel with a Marine guard and doctor in a small craft descending into darkness toward an undisclosed location.

scenes.heading: INT. CABINET ROOM - WHITE HOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney teleconferences from an undisclosed location, demanding to see unvetted intelligence reports and angrily cutting off an intelligence officer who explains their normal vetting process.

scenes.heading: INT. BOMB SHELTER - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: In a bomb shelter, Lynne tells Dick she's scared and he promises to keep her safe, while a metaphorical image shows him fishing with a horrible creature lurking underwater.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOUNTAINS OF AFGHANISTAN - DAY
scenes.summary: US fighter jets bomb mountainsides in Afghanistan with explosive attacks.

scenes.heading: EXT. OLD STONE BUILDING - DAY - 2001
scenes.summary: US soldiers lead Taliban prisoners toward a stone wall as the narrator explains that while Powell and the CIA toppled the Taliban quickly, Cheney had found something more powerful than weapons.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE OF LEGAL COUNSEL - CONFERENCE ROOM - A WEEK LATER
scenes.summary: David Addington introduces John Yoo to Cheney via teleconference at the Office of Legal Counsel, with Addington explaining that the Vice President believes the Commander in Chief's duty to protect the nation supersedes other obligations.
scenes.turningPointNote: The legal framework for expanded executive power and torture begins to take shape

scenes.heading: INT. JOHN YOO’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: John Yoo writes legal opinions at his computer while Addington paces, creating memos that allow warrantless surveillance of citizens and justify torture techniques.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE - OVAL OFFICE
scenes.summary: In the Oval Office, Cheney and Addington explain to Bush and others that the Geneva Convention is open to interpretation, defining enhanced interrogation techniques while claiming the US doesn't torture.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH PRICED GOURMET RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A waiter at a gourmet restaurant formally describes legal concepts like enemy combatants, extreme rendition, and Guantanamo Bay as if they were menu specials to Cheney, Rumsfeld, Wolfowitz and Addington.

scenes.heading: INT. FOCUS GROUP ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: A focus group leader reminds participants they are being recorded and may have clients observing behind a door.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH PRICED GOURMET RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the restaurant, Wolfowitz compliments the focus group results while Rumsfeld notes that people are confused about the war.

scenes.heading: INT. FOCUS GROUP ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Focus group participants express confusion about why they're calling it a war, questioning who they're fighting since they just need to get Osama bin Laden, and asking whether Al Qaeda is a country.

scenes.heading: INT. GOURMET RESTAURANT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The officials discuss how Americans want a country to fight rather than a nebulous enemy, with Cheney concluding it's time to take Iraq as they all smile and laugh.
scenes.turningPointNote: The decision to invade Iraq is made based on political convenience rather than legitimate threats

scenes.heading: 166 INT. OVAL OFFICE - LATER 166 166
scenes.summary: Cheney tells Bush about creating the Office of Special Plans because Tenet isn't serious enough about the Saddam threat, while Bush expresses his desire to take down Saddam.

scenes.heading: 167 INT. PENTAGON - THE OFFICE OF SPECIAL PLANS - DAY 167 167
scenes.summary: At the Pentagon's Office of Special Plans, Douglas Feith reports finding intelligence about hijacker Mohamed Atta possibly meeting with an Iraqi spy in Prague.

scenes.heading: INT. FOCUS GROUP ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Focus group participants debate whether they can just bomb people, with one woman objecting and an older man dismissing her concerns.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTAGON - THE OFFICE OF SPECIAL PLANS - DAY
scenes.summary: Feith notes that Czech intelligence questions the credibility of the Atta report, but Wolfowitz dismisses this and asks who wants to be an unnamed source, instructing them to use the focus-tested phrase about smoking guns and mushroom clouds.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY - SEPT 8 2002
scenes.summary: Condi Rice uses the mushroom cloud phrase in a TV interview with Wolf Blitzer, followed by various politicians across party lines repeating similar talking points about Iraq on different news programs.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - WHITE HOUSE
scenes.summary: In the Oval Office, officials discuss polling showing 53% support for Iraq invasion, with Powell reporting that France, Germany, and Israel won't join the coalition, leading Rove to suggest having Powell address the UN to build support.

scenes.heading: EXT. AFGHANISTAN - AL QAEDA BASE - DAY
scenes.summary: In Afghanistan, Zarqawi meets with Osama bin Laden, but their meeting goes poorly because Zarqawi wants to kill all Shia Muslims and bin Laden's mother was Shia.

scenes.heading: INT. CIA CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney and Scooter Libby question a CIA analyst about Zarqawi's connection to Al Qaeda, with the analyst stating they had no operational contact and Cheney dismissing this as hogwash.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE WEST WING HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Powell storms down a hallway with papers, telling his chief of staff Lawrence Wilkerson that the speech contains five pieces of disproved intelligence and is beyond thin.

scenes.heading: INT. SCOOTER LIBBY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Scooter Libby takes a phone call from Wilkerson while David Addington enters and comments that Powell doesn't have a clue.

scenes.heading: INT. TERRORIST TENT - MIDDLE OF NOWHERE - DAY
scenes.summary: Zarqawi and his followers clean guns in a tent as the narrator explains that after the US invaded Afghanistan, Zarqawi set up in Iraq, providing Cheney's only connection between Al Qaeda and Iraq.

scenes.heading: INT. U.N. GENERAL ASSEMBLY - DAY
scenes.summary: At the UN General Assembly, German Foreign Minister Joschka Fischer introduces Colin Powell, who begins his address about Iraq's weapons of mass destruction and involvement in terrorism.

scenes.heading: INT. SMALL ROOM - IRAQ - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Zarqawi and five terrorists watch Powell's UN speech on a laptop as Powell repeatedly mentions Zarqawi's name, with the narrator explaining this made Zarqawi a star.

scenes.heading: INT. U.N. GENERAL ASSEMBLY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Powell continues his UN address claiming Saddam and bin Laden reached an understanding, with the narrator noting that 70% of Americans came to believe Saddam was involved in 9/11 and that Powell later called this speech the most shameful moment of his life.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Cheney family enjoys a lavish fish dinner together, laughing and talking about American Idol, with Dick telling a joke about stopping fish from smelling.

scenes.heading: EXT. BAGHDAD - MARCH 20, 2003- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Stock footage shows Baghdad's skyline exploding with rockets on March 20, 2003.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Iraq War begins, representing the culmination of Cheney's manipulation and the point of no return

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - WHITE HOUSE
scenes.summary: President Bush addresses the nation from the Oval Office, announcing that American and coalition forces have begun military operations to disarm Iraq and free its people.

scenes.heading: INT. APARTMENT IN BAGHDAD - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: An Iraqi family cowers under a table in their Baghdad apartment as bombs fall around them.

scenes.heading: EXT. BAGHDAD - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: A cruise missile hits the apartment building from a POV video feed perspective.

scenes.heading: EXT. IRAG DESERT - DAY - 2003
scenes.summary: US soldiers run alongside tanks in the Iraqi desert with bodies burning in the street, while crowds of Iraqis pull down Saddam's statue and put an American flag over his head.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRCRAFT CARRIER - APRIL 9, 2003 - DAY
scenes.summary: Stock footage shows President Bush landing on an aircraft carrier in his flight suit on April 9, 2003.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAST WYOMING FIELD - DAY
scenes.summary: The Cheney family rides horses across a vast Wyoming field in western garb, stopping to pose in a magnificent frozen tableau.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAILGATE - DAY
scenes.summary: Pumped up Americans with USA caps and beer chant 'USA! USA! USA!' at a tailgate.

scenes.heading: EXT. IRAQ - DAY
scenes.summary: Kurt the narrator speaks to a video camera from Iraq, explaining that men and equipment are being shipped from Afghanistan to Iraq and he's unsure what's happening.

scenes.heading: EXT. AIRCRAFT CARRIER - APRIL 9, 2003 - DAY
scenes.summary: President Bush speaks from the aircraft carrier podium, declaring that major combat operations in Iraq have ended and the US and allies have prevailed.

scenes.heading: INT. RUMSFELD’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Generals express concerns to Cheney, Rumsfeld, and Wolfowitz about Halliburton KBR's billing practices on no-bid contracts, but Rumsfeld and Cheney dismiss the concerns.

scenes.heading: EXT. TEXAS SKY - DAY - 2006
scenes.summary: During a hunting trip in Texas, Cheney accidentally shoots lawyer Harry Whittington in the face with a shotgun, with the narrator noting that Cheney never apologized for the incident.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney and Scooter Libby review Joe Wilson's op-ed questioning yellowcake intelligence, with Cheney asking about Wilson's wife and ordering Libby to leak that Valerie Plame is undercover CIA.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cheney orders the illegal outing of a CIA operative in retaliation, crossing a major legal and ethical line

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD OUTSIDE OF BAGHDAD - DAY - 2004
scenes.summary: Two soldiers in a Humvee convoy discuss cultural differences about dogs when an explosion goes off under their front wheel.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Rumsfeld storms toward the Vice President's office with a piece of paper, walking past his secretary.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE OF THE VICE PRESIDENT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rumsfeld bursts into Cheney's office with a CIA briefing about Zarqawi leading a major insurgency in Iraq, angry that the report implies it's because they talked him up for months.

scenes.heading: INT. BRITISH SUBWAY CAR - DAY - 2005
scenes.summary: The scene shows a blood-stained London subway car with scattered belongings, a severed hand, and blood smears from dragged bodies, illustrating the carnage Zarqawi wanted to create.

scenes.heading: EXT. SADDAM HUSSEIN’S PALACE - BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: In Saddam Hussein's palace bedroom, a shelf displays dusty VHS tapes of American movies from the 80s, with the narrator revealing that no WMDs were found and Saddam and his sons mostly liked cocaine and American movies.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that no WMDs existed exposes the complete fabrication that justified the war

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE FLOOR - DAY - JUNE 2004
scenes.summary: Cheney walks through the Senate floor shaking hands when Senator Patrick Leahy approaches him about investigating Halliburton contracts, and Cheney responds by telling him to 'go fuck yourself.'

scenes.heading: EXT. NATIONAL’S BALL PARK - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney throws out the first pitch at a baseball game and gets booed by the crowd while the announcer notes his pitch was low and outside.

scenes.heading: INT. PENTAGON HALLWAY - DAY - NOVEMBER 2006
scenes.summary: Cheney calls Rumsfeld to inform him that the President wants him to step down, with Rumsfeld questioning whether it's Bush's decision or Cheney's.
scenes.turningPointNote: Rumsfeld's forced resignation marks the beginning of the administration's collapse

scenes.heading: EXT. WEST FRONT LAWN OF THE CAPITOL - JAN 20, 2009
scenes.summary: Cheney sits in a wheelchair alongside Bush at Barack Obama's inauguration ceremony.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREETS - DAY
scenes.summary: The narrator jogs while explaining that Cheney's heart trouble returned and he found himself at death's door with no donor available.

scenes.heading: INT. GEORGE WASHINGTON UNIVERSITY HOSPITAL - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney lies dying in a hospital bed surrounded by his family as the doctor suggests calling a minister, but Lynne refuses to let him give up.

scenes.heading: EXT. SUBURBAN STREET - DAY
scenes.summary: The narrator is hit by a car while jogging and killed, with quick cuts showing his heart being harvested and prepared for transplant.
scenes.turningPointNote: The narrator's death provides Cheney with a new heart, allowing him to continue living

scenes.heading: INT. SURGICAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Cheney is wheeled into the surgical room for his heart transplant operation.

scenes.heading: INT. MORGUE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The dead narrator lies on a morgue slab and speaks to camera about how his heart will give Cheney another ten years of life.

scenes.heading: INT. SURGICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The surgical team makes an incision in Cheney's chest to begin the heart transplant procedure.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY’S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Liz Cheney breaks down in tears after hearing a recorded push poll attacking her support for gay marriage that went to every house in Wyoming.

scenes.heading: INT. SURGICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The surgeon opens Cheney's chest with a spreader, revealing his failing heart inside.

scenes.heading: INT. NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Liz Cheney appears on a news show where she's questioned about her stance on gay marriage and her sister's relationship.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY’S TV ROOM - VIRGINIA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cheney and Lynne watch Liz's television interview, with Lynne commenting that she thinks Liz is coming off well.

scenes.heading: INT. MARY CHENEY'S HOME - THE NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Mary Cheney calls her parents in tears, expressing disbelief that they would approve of Liz's political stance against her marriage.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY’S VIRGINIA HOME - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Lynne dismisses Mary as hysterical during their phone conversation while Dick sits silently in the background.

scenes.heading: INT. SURGICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The surgeon removes Cheney's damaged heart, leaving a hole in his chest, followed by quick cuts showing the devastation of war and political division.

scenes.heading: INT. SURGICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A new heart is placed into Cheney's chest cavity during the transplant surgery.

scenes.heading: INT. CHENEY’S LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Liz waits for her father's response about the push poll attacking her sister, and after a beat, Dick nods his approval to proceed.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cheney's decision to sacrifice his daughter Mary for Liz's political ambitions reveals his ultimate prioritization of power over family

scenes.heading: INT. MARY’S HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Mary cries while her wife comforts her, followed by quick cuts showing various images of social and political turmoil.

scenes.heading: EXT. WYOMING LAKE SIDE - DAY
scenes.summary: The camera pushes through trees to focus on an old carving of a heart with 'Dick + Lynne' on a tree trunk.

scenes.heading: INT. SURGICAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cheney's heart surgery concludes successfully with stable vital signs, while text reveals the family's current status and Mary's refusal to discuss her relationship with Liz.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW ROOM
scenes.summary: Cheney sits down for an interview with Martha and the camera crew, making small talk about the setup while statistics about war casualties appear on screen.

scenes.heading: INT. INTERVIEW ROOM
scenes.summary: Cheney dismisses public opinion about the Iraq War and addresses the camera directly, defending his actions and refusing to apologize for his decisions.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cheney's final unrepentant monologue serves as his ultimate statement of defiance and lack of remorse

scenes.heading: INT. FOCUS GROUP ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The focus group erupts into a heated political argument between Mark and Turner about liberal bias, with Mark calling Turner names and both sides becoming increasingly hostile.

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
